steps.title: Identify what y-intercept means
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis. This occurs when $x = 0$. The y-intercept is always written as an ordered pair $(0, y)$.

steps.title: Read the intersection point from the graph
steps.content: Locate $x = 0$ on the graph (the vertical y-axis). Follow the curve to where it crosses this axis. From the graph, the curve intersects the y-axis at the point $(0, 5)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
